description: Maps every area of the repository to the code it owns and the documentation that governs it, so 'which docs govern this change?' always has a machine-readable answer. This file is committed data guarded by an admission test: scripts/check-area-map.js fails CI when the map goes stale (a pattern matching no tracked file, an entry pointing at an untracked file), when a tracked file resolves to no area and is neither a repo-wide doc nor a justified exception, or when any tracked .md doc (anywhere in the repo, not only under docs/) is placed in no area's doc set, repo-wide list, or justified 'unassigned' exception — being matched only by a code pattern is not a doc home. An area owns code, a doc set, or both: a docs-only area (no code) is valid when it carries a non-empty doc set. Code patterns support '*', '**', and {a,b} alternation only, and every brace alternative must match at least one tracked file. Doc entries are literal tracked paths, never patterns. Code files and docs are many-to-many: a file or doc may belong to several areas. A code file may also name a governing doc directly with a '// see docs/<path>.md' comment, which adds every area whose doc set contains that doc.

repo-wide.description: Docs that apply to every area of the repository — a change anywhere can be governed by them. They resolve in their own right and do not need to belong to an area's doc set (though some also do).

unassigned.reason: License text — not project documentation and owned by no area.
Contributor license agreement — project governance, not area documentation.
Shipped end-user assets (icons, the reading guide delivered with dispatch payloads) — product artifacts, not project documentation.

Security policy — coordinated-disclosure governance (how a vulnerability is reported and fixed), referenced from .github/CONTRIBUTING.md; a process/governance doc, not code-governing area documentation.
Code of Conduct (Contributor Covenant) — community governance, not area documentation.
Pull-request body template — contribution scaffolding whose rules live in .github/CONTRIBUTING.md; a form, not area documentation.
Reference-implementations index — orientation for runnable example artifacts (never shipped in a release), not project doctrine.
Reference sync-server README — implementation notes for a runnable example artifact (never shipped); the protocol it implements is governed by docs/api/sync-protocol.md.
